mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and consumption demand, translating into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following a month-over-month increase in PAYEMS is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% tightens financial conditions, increases debt-service costs, and reduces equity risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days while the rate remains above this threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a low nominal federal funds rate compress real interest rates, stimulating borrowing and reflation trades that lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and the level of FEDFUNDS fails to generate a rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the 60-day forward return following joint high-inflation and low-rate conditions is non-positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal accelerating residential investment and construction employment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as post-recession recovery, month-over-month HOUST increases are followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures consumer spending momentum, which feeds into near-term corporate revenue surprises and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average 5-day forward return following RSAFS increases is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month span indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that compress household income expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following a 0.5pp UNRATE increase over 3 months are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return during these episodes is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a low federal funds rate indicates robust supply-side output growth financed by cheap credit, jointly lifting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from INDPRO acceleration and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive average 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In high-unemployment environments, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal an imminent cyclical rebound in construction and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, month-over-month HOUST increases are followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive forward returns falls below 40%.
